free_text: Comprehensive study of reaction mechanisms for the 9-Be
+ 144-Sm system at near- and sub-barrier energies
Five 88.6% enriched Sm-144 targets were used with
thicknesses from 170 to 220-mug/cm**2 on carbon backing
of 20-mug/cm**2. An aluminum catcher foil was placed a
few mm behind target. A catcher foil thickness of
1.6-mg/cm**2 was chosen, such that it should stop the
fusion residues but let the elastically scattered beam
particles pass through.
The elastic and inelastic scattering cross
sections were measured with a set of seven silicon
surface barrier detectors, placed at 40-cm from the
target. The angular separation between two adjacent
detectors was 5 degree.
An additional surface barrier detector used as monitor
was placed at 31.6 degrees, relative to the beam
direction, for normalization purposes.
A germanium detector with an energy resolution
of 600 eV for evaporation residue production cross
section.
Different processes following the breakup should be
considered: noncapture breakup (NCBU), when neither of
the  breakup fragments is captured by the target;
incomplete fusion reaction (ICF), when one of the
fragments is captured by the target; sequential
complete fusion(SCF), when breakup occurs followed by
the successive capture of all fragments by the target.
In summary, the total breakup cross section is sum
of three contribution-NCBU, ICF and SCF.
The sum of complete fusion (including two body
fusion and complete fusion following breakup) and
incomplete fusion is called total fusion.

*By compiler*.Authors regard all Gd-147 yield as
product from incomplete fusion. The other two possible
products, Gd-146 and Gd-148 are not measured in this
experiment because of long-lifes of these two nuclides.
